channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Suspended South Korean President Yoon to skip first day of impeachment trial over safety concerns
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol will not attend the first day of his impeachment trial this week, according to his lawyer, who cited concerns for the suspended president’s safety.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: France's battered Mayotte islands hit by a new tropical storm just weeks after a devastating cyclone
item.description: The French territory of Mayotte is on red alert again with another cyclone heading toward the islands off Africa that were devastated by their worst storm in nearly a century last month.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Germany says sanctions against Syrian war crimes suspects must stay but people need relief
item.description: Germany’s foreign minister says sanctions against Syrian officials responsible for war crimes must remain in place but called for a “smart approach” to provide relief to the Syrian population after last month’s overthrow of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: Baraa Anwer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Croatia's President Milanović overwhelming favorite to win reelection in a runoff vote
item.description: Croatia’s President Zoran Milanović is the overwhelming favorite to win reelection against a candidate from the ruling conservative party in a runoff presidential vote.
item.dc:creator: Sasa Kavic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mayotte on red alert again as another cyclone heads toward the French territory off Africa
item.description: The French territory of Mayotte is on red alert again with another cyclone heading toward the islands off Africa that were devastated by their worst storm in nearly a century last month.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trams collide in the French city of Strasbourg, injuring dozens, though none critically
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Two trams collided in Strasbourg in eastern France on Saturday, causing dozens of injuries, though none critical, authorities said. The accident took place during the afternoon in a tunnel leading to the station near the city's central train station.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trams collide in the French city of Strasbourg, injuring dozens, though none critically
item.description: Firefighters say two trams have collided in Strasbourg in eastern France, injuring dozens of people, though none critically.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu sends Mossad director to Gaza ceasefire talks in Qatar in sign of progress
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is sending the director of the Mossad foreign intelligence agency to ceasefire negotiations in Qatar in a sign of progress in talks on the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine says it captured 2 North Korean soldiers fighting for Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s forces have captured two North Korean soldiers fighting alongside Russian forces in Russia’s Kursk border region, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Saturday, the first such claim by Kyiv since North Korea sent thousands of troops to shore up Moscow's war effort on the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey's Kurdish leaders meet jailed politician as the two sides inch toward peace
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A delegation from one of Turkey's biggest pro-Kurdish political parties met a leading figure of the Kurdish movement in prison Saturday, the latest step in a tentative process to end the country’s 40-year conflict, the party said.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sudan’s military retakes a strategic city from RSF rebels, dealing them a major blow
item.description: Sudanese officials says the military and its allies had taken back the strategic city of Wad Medani more than a year after the Rapid Support Forces seized it.
item.dc:creator: By Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Black boxes from South Korea plane crash failed to record final 4 minutes, officials say
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The black boxes of a Boeing jetliner that crashed in South Korea last month stopped recording about four minutes before the accident, South Korean officials said Saturday, possibly complicating investigations into the cause of the disaster that killed 179 people.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian intelligence agency says it thwarted a planned Islamic State attack on a Shiite shrine
item.description: Intelligence officials in Syria’s new de facto government say they have thwarted a plan by the Islamic State group to set off a bomb at a Shiite shrine in the Damascus suburb of Sayyida Zeinab.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Venezuela latest: Maduro sworn in for third term as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela's Maduro takes new oath amid protests and international rebuke
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a new term on Friday, extending his increasingly repressive rule in the face of renewed protests and rebukes from the United States and others who believe he stole last year's vote.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel launches new strikes on Yemen’s Houthi rebels
item.description: Israel’s military says it carried out new airstrikes in Yemen against what it said were Houthi rebel targets. Its statement Friday said fighter jets struck “on the western coast and inland Yemen,” a day after the Houthis launched three drones at Israel. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lynx on the loose in Scotland highlight debate over reintroducing species into the wild
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Scottish environmental activists want to reintroduce the lynx into the forests of the Highlands. But not this way.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration imposes major sanctions on Russian energy sector
item.description: The Biden administration announced Friday it was imposing what it billed as the most significant sanctions to date on the Russian energy sector, targeting Moscow’s oil and liquified natural gas (LNG) exports just weeks before leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party to decide on March 9 who will succeed Trudeau as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's governing Liberal Party will announce the country's next prime minister on March 9 after a leadership vote that follows the resignation of Justin Trudeau this week, party leaders said late Thursday. Trudeau remains prime minister until a new leader is chosen.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — It’s a reality show about a loving couple waiting to welcome their new arrival, watched by thousands of ardent fans.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanon's parliament elects army commander Joseph Aoun as president, ending a 2-year deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s parliament voted Thursday to elect army commander Joseph Aoun as head of state, filling a more than two-year-long presidential vacuum.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU ALJOUD and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign policy chief says Trump is right to urge higher military spending from NATO
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Europe’s security “is on a knife-edge” and President-elect Donald Trump is right to say NATO member nations must increase military spending, Britain’s top diplomat said Thursday. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said that “the post-Cold War peace is well and truly over.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Details of Venezuelan opposition leader's possible arrest remain unclear amid Maduro inauguration resistance
item.description: Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's aides claim she was detained Thursday, despite denials by the country's socialist government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poland adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz liberation commemoration
item.description: Poland passed a resolution to ensure the safe participation of Israeli representatives who attend 80th anniversary commemorations of the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia monitoring Trump’s ‘dramatic’ comments on Greenland acquisition
item.description: The Kremlin said Thursday they are monitoring President-elect Donald Trump's "dramatic" comments over his desire to acquire Greenland amid other expansionist remarks, including the Panama Canal and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel sends report to UN on 'brutal' treatment used by terrorists against hostages in Gaza
item.description: In a new report sent to the U.N. by Israel's health ministry, freed hostages reveal systematic abuse by Hamas, as 99 remain in captivity enduring brutal physical and psychological torment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The historic importance of Greenland for US national security as debate over island's future roars on
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's recent comments on Greenland has put the world's largest island, a district of Denmark, to the top of many foreign policy discussions. Experts weigh in on its importance to US national security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby girl born on packed migrant boat heading for Spain's Canary Islands
item.description: A baby girl was born on a packed migrant boat headed for Spain's Lanzarote island in the Canary Islands. Authorities said the baby and her mother were in good condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Americans arrested in Venezuela on eve of Maduro inauguration over ‘terrorism’ claims
item.description: Two Americans have been arrested in Venezuela, with President Nicolas Maduro claiming the charges relate to an intent to "practice terrorism against the Venezuelan people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation
